steps.title: Identify the contrast signal
steps.content: The word “Yet” contrasts with the previous sentence's classification of Washoe and Chitimacha as isolates. The underlined sentence proposes these isolates are “potentially remnants of families whose other members vanished”—meaning they once belonged to families.

steps.title: Determine what this temporal shift implies
steps.content:
Choice D correctly proposes that the present-day isolate/nonisolate distinction may not have applied in the distant past.

The other three either reverse the causation, invoke mechanisms not mentioned, or misconstrue the point as being about historical record bias rather than temporal reclassification.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
